program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the primary purpose of a needs assessment before designing a program?
options: To identify the gap between current conditions and desired outcomes
To secure funding for the program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To identify the gap between current conditions and desired outcomes

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which of the following best describes a program goal?
options: A broad statement of the intended long-term result
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A broad statement of the intended long-term result

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How do objectives differ from goals in program planning?
options: Objectives are specific and measurable steps toward broader goals
Objectives are more general than goals
Objectives describe the mission while goals describe tasks
Objectives are optional while goals are required
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Objectives are specific and measurable steps toward broader goals

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should objectives be measurable?
options: So progress and success can be assessed objectively
So they can be changed easily
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: So progress and success can be assessed objectively

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the main purpose of a pilot program?
options: To test a program on a small scale before full implementation
To replace evaluation of the full program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To test a program on a small scale before full implementation

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What distinguishes strategic planning from operational planning?
options: Strategic planning sets long-term direction, while operational planning details how day-to-day work will achieve it
Strategic planning covers daily tasks, while operational planning sets the mission
They are two names for the same activity
Operational planning is done only by outside consultants
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Strategic planning sets long-term direction, while operational planning details how day-to-day work will achieve it

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is formative evaluation primarily used for?
options: Improving a program while it is being developed or implemented
Judging a program only after it ends
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Improving a program while it is being developed or implemented

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is summative evaluation primarily used for?
options: Assessing a program's overall effectiveness after implementation
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Assessing a program's overall effectiveness after implementation

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does a process evaluation examine?
options: Whether program activities are being delivered as intended
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Whether program activities are being delivered as intended

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does an outcome evaluation examine?
options: Whether the program produced its intended results
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Whether the program produced its intended results

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is baseline data important in program evaluation?
options: It provides a point of comparison for measuring change
It replaces the need for follow-up data
It reduces the cost of evaluation
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It provides a point of comparison for measuring change

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which question does a cost-effectiveness analysis primarily address?
options: Whether results are achieved at a reasonable cost relative to alternatives
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Whether results are achieved at a reasonable cost relative to alternatives

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the first logical step in the planning process?
options: Defining the problem or need the plan will address
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Defining the problem or need the plan will address

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should stakeholders be involved in program planning?
options: Their input improves the plan's relevance and acceptance
It transfers responsibility away from planners
It is required only for large programs
It speeds up planning by avoiding discussion
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Their input improves the plan's relevance and acceptance

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a performance indicator?
options: A measurable value used to track progress toward an objective
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A measurable value used to track progress toward an objective

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of setting priorities among program activities?
options: To direct limited resources to the most important activities
To ensure every activity gets equal resources
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To direct limited resources to the most important activities

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: When implementing a new procedure, why is staff training important?
options: It ensures staff understand and can correctly apply the procedure
It replaces the need for written procedures
It reduces the number of staff needed
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It ensures staff understand and can correctly apply the procedure

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a common reason new programs fail during implementation?
options: Inadequate planning for resources, training, or communication
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Inadequate planning for resources, training, or communication

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should a plan include a timeline?
options: It sequences activities and allows progress to be monitored
It guarantees deadlines will be met
It replaces the need for objectives
It is required only for federal programs
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It sequences activities and allows progress to be monitored

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is contingency planning?
options: Preparing alternative courses of action in case problems arise
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Preparing alternative courses of action in case problems arise

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What role does monitoring play during program implementation?
options: It tracks progress so problems can be detected and corrected early
It is needed only when problems occur
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It tracks progress so problems can be detected and corrected early

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which best describes an evaluation criterion?
options: A standard used to judge a program's performance
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A standard used to judge a program's performance

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is it important to define evaluation criteria before a program begins?
options: So data needed to judge success can be collected from the start
So the evaluation can be skipped later
So criteria can be adjusted to guarantee success
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: So data needed to judge success can be collected from the start

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the relationship between planning and evaluation?
options: Evaluation results inform future planning in a continuous cycle
Planning eliminates the need for evaluation
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Evaluation results inform future planning in a continuous cycle

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does feasibility refer to in program planning?
options: Whether a proposed program can realistically be carried out with available resources
Whether a program is popular with staff
Whether a program has a name
Whether a program has been done elsewhere
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Whether a proposed program can realistically be carried out with available resources

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A program achieved its objectives but at far higher cost than planned. What does this suggest?
options: The program was effective but not efficient
The program was efficient but not effective
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The program was effective but not efficient

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the benefit of clearly assigning responsibility for each task in a plan?
options: Accountability is established and tasks are less likely to be overlooked
Tasks can be completed in any order
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Accountability is established and tasks are less likely to be overlooked

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should evaluation findings be reported even when results are disappointing?
options: Accurate findings support learning and better future decisions
Negative findings can be hidden legally
Reports are only for successful programs
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Accurate findings support learning and better future decisions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the main purpose of a program budget in planning?
options: To estimate and allocate the resources needed to carry out activities
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To estimate and allocate the resources needed to carry out activities

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is an input in a program logic model?
options: A resource such as staff, funding, or facilities used by the program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A resource such as staff, funding, or facilities used by the program

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is an output in a program logic model?
options: A direct product of program activities, such as services delivered
The funding received by the program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A direct product of program activities, such as services delivered

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is an outcome in a program logic model?
options: A change or benefit resulting from the program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A change or benefit resulting from the program

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why might a program with high outputs still have poor outcomes?
options: Delivering many services does not guarantee they produce the intended change
Outputs and outcomes are the same thing
High outputs always mean poor quality
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Delivering many services does not guarantee they produce the intended change

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does it mean to implement a procedure incrementally?
options: To introduce it in stages rather than all at once
To apply it only to management
To document it after the fact
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To introduce it in stages rather than all at once

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why are long-range plans generally less detailed than short-range plans?
options: Uncertainty increases over time, so distant activities cannot be specified precisely
Detail is prohibited in long-range plans
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Uncertainty increases over time, so distant activities cannot be specified precisely

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: When evaluating a program, why is it important to consider unintended effects?
options: Programs can produce significant consequences beyond their stated objectives
It reduces the amount of data needed
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Programs can produce significant consequences beyond their stated objectives

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of a status report during implementation?
options: To keep decision-makers informed of progress, problems, and next steps
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To keep decision-makers informed of progress, problems, and next steps

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which is the best example of a measurable objective?
options: Reduce average application processing time from ten days to five days within one year
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Reduce average application processing time from ten days to five days within one year

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should a plan identify required resources early?
options: Shortfalls can be addressed before they delay implementation
Resources are only needed at the end
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Shortfalls can be addressed before they delay implementation

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of comparing actual results to planned targets?
options: To identify variances that may require corrective action
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To identify variances that may require corrective action

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A manager finds that a new program is behind schedule. What should be done first?
options: Determine the causes of the delay before deciding on corrective action
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Determine the causes of the delay before deciding on corrective action

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the value of documenting lessons learned at the end of a program?
options: Future programs can benefit from the experience gained
It is required only for failed programs
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Future programs can benefit from the experience gained

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which factor is most important when selecting among alternative program designs?
options: How well each alternative achieves objectives within available resources
Which alternative is easiest to name
Which alternative requires the least explanation
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: How well each alternative achieves objectives within available resources

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does program efficiency measure?
options: The relationship between resources used and outputs produced
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The relationship between resources used and outputs produced

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is a clear statement of the problem important before proposing solutions?
options: Solutions are only effective if they address the actual problem
Problems are more interesting than solutions
It removes the need for data
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Solutions are only effective if they address the actual problem

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the role of data collection in program monitoring?
options: It provides the factual basis for tracking progress and making decisions
It is done only if problems occur
It is useful only for large programs
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It provides the factual basis for tracking progress and making decisions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: When new procedures are introduced, resistance from staff is best reduced by:
options: Explaining the reasons for the change and involving staff in implementation
Penalizing anyone who questions the change
Keeping the change secret as long as possible
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Explaining the reasons for the change and involving staff in implementation

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the primary reason to evaluate a program's effectiveness?
options: To determine whether it achieves its intended results
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To determine whether it achieves its intended results

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is benchmarking in program management?
options: Comparing performance against standards or similar programs
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Comparing performance against standards or similar programs

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should evaluation methods match the questions being asked?
options: Different questions require different types of evidence
Methods are chosen for convenience only
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Different questions require different types of evidence

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A program's objectives were achieved, but the target population was not reached. What does this indicate?
options: The program may have served the wrong group despite meeting numeric targets
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The program may have served the wrong group despite meeting numeric targets

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of interim milestones in a project timeline?
options: They allow progress to be checked at defined points before the deadline
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They allow progress to be checked at defined points before the deadline

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What should be done when monitoring shows an activity is not working as intended?
options: Analyze the cause and adjust the approach
Continue unchanged to preserve the plan
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Analyze the cause and adjust the approach

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the main advantage of written implementation procedures?
options: They promote consistent application by all staff
They reduce the need for objectives
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They promote consistent application by all staff

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which best describes evaluation objectivity?
options: Judging results based on evidence rather than personal preference
Choosing data that supports the desired conclusion
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Judging results based on evidence rather than personal preference

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is it useful to state assumptions in a program plan?
options: If assumptions prove false, the plan can be revisited with that understanding
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: If assumptions prove false, the plan can be revisited with that understanding

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does program feasibility depend on most directly?
options: Available staff, funding, time, and organizational capacity
The color of the report cover
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Available staff, funding, time, and organizational capacity

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the best use of evaluation findings that show partial success?
options: Strengthen effective components and revise or replace weak ones
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Strengthen effective components and revise or replace weak ones

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should performance targets be realistic?
options: Unrealistic targets can discourage staff and distort reporting
Targets should always be impossible to reach
Realistic targets eliminate the need to monitor
Targets have no effect on behavior
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Unrealistic targets can discourage staff and distort reporting

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of a work plan?
options: To specify tasks, responsibilities, timelines, and resources for reaching objectives
To announce the program to the public
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To specify tasks, responsibilities, timelines, and resources for reaching objectives

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A new program requires cooperation from another unit. What planning step addresses this?
options: Coordinating with the other unit early and defining shared responsibilities
Excluding the other unit from planning
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Coordinating with the other unit early and defining shared responsibilities

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the value of periodic progress reviews?
options: They provide regular opportunities to correct course before small issues grow
They are useful only for failing programs
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They provide regular opportunities to correct course before small issues grow

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How does clear communication support implementation of new procedures?
options: Staff who understand what is expected are more likely to comply correctly
It replaces the need for training
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Staff who understand what is expected are more likely to comply correctly

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the difference between monitoring and evaluation?
options: Monitoring tracks ongoing progress; evaluation judges merit or effectiveness
Evaluation happens daily; monitoring happens once
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Monitoring tracks ongoing progress; evaluation judges merit or effectiveness

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is participant feedback useful in program evaluation?
options: It provides direct evidence about how the program is experienced
It is collected only from staff
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It provides direct evidence about how the program is experienced

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the risk of evaluating a program too soon after implementation?
options: Outcomes may not have had time to develop, understating effectiveness
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Outcomes may not have had time to develop, understating effectiveness

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the function of standards in maintaining program quality?
options: They define acceptable performance against which work can be checked
They apply only to new staff
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They define acceptable performance against which work can be checked

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: When resources are cut, what is the most rational planning response?
options: Reassess priorities and adjust the scope of activities accordingly
Continue all activities at reduced quality
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Reassess priorities and adjust the scope of activities accordingly

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the primary purpose of comparing program alternatives before selection?
options: To choose the option most likely to achieve objectives efficiently
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To choose the option most likely to achieve objectives efficiently

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does sustainability refer to in program planning?
options: The ability of a program's benefits or operations to continue over time
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The ability of a program's benefits or operations to continue over time

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which is a sign that program objectives may need revision?
options: Conditions have changed so the objectives no longer address current needs
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Conditions have changed so the objectives no longer address current needs

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the benefit of involving those who will use a procedure in designing it?
options: Their practical knowledge helps create workable procedures and builds acceptance
It slows the design process unnecessarily
It shifts blame if the procedure fails
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Their practical knowledge helps create workable procedures and builds acceptance

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What should an implementation schedule account for besides task durations?
options: Dependencies among tasks and availability of resources
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Dependencies among tasks and availability of resources

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of defining a program's target population?
options: To ensure services are designed for and reach the intended group
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To ensure services are designed for and reach the intended group

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A program shows different results across locations. What should evaluators do?
options: Examine what factors differ across locations to explain the variation
Average the results and ignore differences
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Examine what factors differ across locations to explain the variation

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why are clear definitions important when collecting program data?
options: Consistent definitions make data comparable and meaningful
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Consistent definitions make data comparable and meaningful

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the main purpose of a program's mission or purpose statement?
options: To provide overall direction that guides goals and activities
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To provide overall direction that guides goals and activities

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How can evaluation findings improve efficiency?
options: By identifying activities that consume resources without adding results
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: By identifying activities that consume resources without adding results

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the most appropriate response to evaluation findings that conflict with expectations?
options: Examine the evidence carefully before drawing conclusions
Change the findings to match expectations
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Examine the evidence carefully before drawing conclusions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does coordination among units during implementation prevent?
options: Duplication of effort and conflicting actions
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Duplication of effort and conflicting actions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which element should a corrective action plan include?
options: Specific steps, responsible parties, and target dates for fixing the problem
Only a description of the problem
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Specific steps, responsible parties, and target dates for fixing the problem

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why might a phased rollout include a review point between phases?
options: To decide whether to proceed, adjust, or halt based on results so far
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To decide whether to proceed, adjust, or halt based on results so far

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the danger of measuring only what is easy to measure?
options: Important results that are harder to measure may be neglected
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Important results that are harder to measure may be neglected

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a reasonable first response when a new procedure produces unexpected errors?
options: Investigate whether the cause is the procedure itself, training, or application
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Investigate whether the cause is the procedure itself, training, or application

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of establishing reporting requirements at program start?
options: To ensure needed information flows regularly to those overseeing the program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To ensure needed information flows regularly to those overseeing the program

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How does a clear chain of decision-making authority help implementation?
options: Questions and problems can be resolved quickly by the right person
It removes the need for plans
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Questions and problems can be resolved quickly by the right person

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is meant by program scope?
options: The boundaries of what the program will and will not do
The physical size of the office
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The boundaries of what the program will and will not do

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is scope control important during implementation?
options: Uncontrolled expansion of activities can exhaust resources and delay objectives
Scope has no effect on resources
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Uncontrolled expansion of activities can exhaust resources and delay objectives

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the role of evaluation in accountability?
options: It provides evidence of what was accomplished with the resources provided
It applies only to private organizations
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It provides evidence of what was accomplished with the resources provided

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A planner must choose between two effective options; one costs significantly less. What principle favors the cheaper option?
options: Efficiency — achieving results with fewer resources
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Efficiency — achieving results with fewer resources

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should evaluation reports distinguish findings from recommendations?
options: Findings state what the evidence shows; recommendations state proposed actions
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Findings state what the evidence shows; recommendations state proposed actions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which practice supports objective interpretation of program data?
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Comparing results against pre-established criteria

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the benefit of realistic scheduling over optimistic scheduling?
options: Plans based on realistic estimates are more likely to be met and trusted
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Plans based on realistic estimates are more likely to be met and trusted

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of an evaluability assessment?
options: To determine whether a program is ready and able to be meaningfully evaluated
To calculate the final program score
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To determine whether a program is ready and able to be meaningfully evaluated

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does it mean for program activities to be aligned with objectives?
options: Each activity contributes logically to achieving the stated objectives
Activities occur at the same time
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Each activity contributes logically to achieving the stated objectives

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a key question in assessing program relevance?
options: Does the program address a real and current need?
Does the program have a slogan?
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Does the program address a real and current need?

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How can regular team meetings support implementation?
options: They surface problems early and keep efforts coordinated
They are useful only at the end
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They surface problems early and keep efforts coordinated

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a control in the planning-organizing-controlling cycle?
options: A mechanism for checking performance against plans and correcting deviations
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A mechanism for checking performance against plans and correcting deviations

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is flexibility valuable in a program plan?
options: Conditions change, and plans must adapt to remain effective
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Conditions change, and plans must adapt to remain effective

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which statement about evaluation timing is most accurate?
options: Different evaluation types are appropriate at different program stages
All evaluation happens at the end
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Different evaluation types are appropriate at different program stages

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the central idea of management by objectives?
options: Supervisors and staff jointly set measurable objectives and assess performance against them
Performance is judged on effort alone
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Supervisors and staff jointly set measurable objectives and assess performance against them

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A program's demand has doubled. What planning concern arises first?
options: Whether current resources and capacity can meet the increased demand
Whether the program name should change
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Whether current resources and capacity can meet the increased demand

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should success criteria be agreed upon by key parties in advance?
options: It prevents later disputes about whether the program succeeded
Criteria are best decided after results
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It prevents later disputes about whether the program succeeded

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is triangulation in evaluation?
options: Using multiple sources or methods to strengthen confidence in findings
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Using multiple sources or methods to strengthen confidence in findings

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does a schedule chart that displays each task as a bar across a calendar primarily show?
options: When each activity starts and ends and how activities overlap in time
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: When each activity starts and ends and how activities overlap in time

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the most useful characteristic of a progress measure?
options: It reflects meaningful movement toward the objective
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It reflects meaningful movement toward the objective

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What should happen to a plan after a major assumption proves false?
options: The affected parts of the plan should be reviewed and revised
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The affected parts of the plan should be reviewed and revised

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is over-reliance on a single data source risky in evaluation?
options: Errors or bias in that source can distort conclusions
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Errors or bias in that source can distort conclusions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which is an example of an efficiency measure?
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Cost per client served

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of an organizational readiness assessment before change?
options: To determine whether staff, systems, and resources can support the change
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To determine whether staff, systems, and resources can support the change

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: When is a proxy indicator appropriately used?
options: When the outcome of interest cannot be measured directly and a related measure must stand in for it
When direct measurement is easy and cheap
When results must be kept private
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: When the outcome of interest cannot be measured directly and a related measure must stand in for it

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the main risk of skipping the planning phase?
options: Efforts may be misdirected, wasting resources on the wrong activities
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Efforts may be misdirected, wasting resources on the wrong activities

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of scanning internal capacities and external conditions before planning?
options: To ground the plan in a realistic picture of strengths, weaknesses, and outside factors that affect success
To delay planning as long as possible
To eliminate the need for objectives
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To ground the plan in a realistic picture of strengths, weaknesses, and outside factors that affect success

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How should limited evaluation resources be focused?
options: On the questions most important for decisions about the program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: On the questions most important for decisions about the program

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does it mean to operationalize an objective?
options: To define it in concrete, measurable terms that guide action
To assign it to operations staff
To remove it from the plan
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To define it in concrete, measurable terms that guide action

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which describes a realistic approach to program risks?
options: Identify likely risks and prepare responses proportional to their impact
Treat all risks as equally likely
Address risks only after they occur
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Identify likely risks and prepare responses proportional to their impact

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the value of comparing a program's results with a similar group not served by the program?
options: It helps determine whether changes are attributable to the program
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It helps determine whether changes are attributable to the program

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should reporting formats be standardized across program sites?
options: Standardization allows results to be combined and compared meaningfully
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Standardization allows results to be combined and compared meaningfully

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the primary function of a steering or oversight group for a major program?
options: To provide direction, review progress, and resolve major issues
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To provide direction, review progress, and resolve major issues

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which situation suggests a program should be modified rather than expanded?
options: Evaluation shows weak results in its current form
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Evaluation shows weak results in its current form

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of exit criteria for a pilot phase?
options: To define what results justify moving to broader implementation
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To define what results justify moving to broader implementation

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How do written objectives support staff performance?
options: They clarify what staff efforts should accomplish
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They clarify what staff efforts should accomplish

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does an evaluation design specify?
options: What will be measured, how, when, and against what comparison
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: What will be measured, how, when, and against what comparison

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A manager wants to know whether a training program changed job behavior. Which evidence is most relevant?
options: Observations or measures of on-the-job performance after training
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Observations or measures of on-the-job performance after training

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is it useful to rank problems by importance during planning?
options: Resources can be directed to problems with the greatest impact
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Resources can be directed to problems with the greatest impact

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does continuity planning ensure for an ongoing program?
options: Essential operations can continue despite disruptions such as staff turnover
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Essential operations can continue despite disruptions such as staff turnover

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the main advantage of setting interim targets for a multi-year objective?
options: Progress can be assessed and adjusted annually rather than only at the end
Interim targets replace the final objective
They are required by all budgets
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Progress can be assessed and adjusted annually rather than only at the end

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which best describes stakeholder analysis?
options: Identifying who is affected by a program and understanding their interests
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Identifying who is affected by a program and understanding their interests

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the appropriate role of judgment in evaluation?
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Interpreting evidence within established criteria

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why might an agency conduct a follow-up evaluation years after a program ends?
options: To assess whether outcomes were sustained over time
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To assess whether outcomes were sustained over time

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the risk of implementing a program before defining its objectives?
options: There is no clear basis for guiding activities or judging success
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: There is no clear basis for guiding activities or judging success

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does resource leveling address in planning?
options: Scheduling work so resource demands stay within available capacity
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Scheduling work so resource demands stay within available capacity

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which is the best indicator that a new procedure has been institutionalized?
options: Staff follow it consistently as part of normal operations without special oversight
It was announced at a meeting
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Staff follow it consistently as part of normal operations without special oversight

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does economy refer to in assessing program operations?
options: Acquiring needed resources of appropriate quality at the lowest reasonable cost
Spending the entire budget each year
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Acquiring needed resources of appropriate quality at the lowest reasonable cost

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should evaluation limitations be stated in reports?
options: Readers can judge how much confidence to place in the findings
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Readers can judge how much confidence to place in the findings

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which question does an impact evaluation attempt to answer?
options: What difference did the program make compared with what would have happened without it?
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: What difference did the program make compared with what would have happened without it?

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the value of a clearly defined decision point in a phased plan?
options: Leadership can commit resources stage by stage based on demonstrated results
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Leadership can commit resources stage by stage based on demonstrated results

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does it mean when a program is described as scalable?
options: It can expand to serve more people without losing effectiveness
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It can expand to serve more people without losing effectiveness

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How does defining roles and responsibilities prevent implementation problems?
options: It reduces confusion about who performs and decides what
It removes the need for skills
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It reduces confusion about who performs and decides what

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the best basis for deciding whether to continue, modify, or end a program?
options: Evidence about its effectiveness, efficiency, and continued relevance
The preference of the loudest advocate
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Evidence about its effectiveness, efficiency, and continued relevance

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should data collection burdens be kept reasonable?
options: Excessive burden reduces data quality and takes time from services
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Excessive burden reduces data quality and takes time from services

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the function of an implementation checklist?
options: To verify that required steps are completed before and during rollout
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To verify that required steps are completed before and during rollout

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A proposed program duplicates services already offered elsewhere. What planning principle is violated?
options: Coordination — resources should complement, not duplicate, existing services
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Coordination — resources should complement, not duplicate, existing services

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the role of feedback loops in program management?
options: Information about results is returned to decision-makers to guide adjustments
Feedback is collected but never used
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Information about results is returned to decision-makers to guide adjustments

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which best describes a SMART objective?
options: Specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is achievability an important quality of objectives?
options: Objectives beyond available capacity set the program up for failure
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Objectives beyond available capacity set the program up for failure

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a logic model used for?
options: Showing how resources and activities are expected to lead to outcomes
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Showing how resources and activities are expected to lead to outcomes

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does 'time-bound' mean in objective setting?
options: The objective specifies when it should be accomplished
The objective is timed with a stopwatch
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The objective specifies when it should be accomplished

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which activity belongs to the controlling function of management?
options: Comparing results with plans and correcting significant deviations
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Comparing results with plans and correcting significant deviations

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the risk of setting too many priorities?
options: Effort is spread so thin that nothing receives adequate attention
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Effort is spread so thin that nothing receives adequate attention

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should program plans reference the resources actually authorized rather than hoped for?
options: Plans based on unavailable resources cannot be executed reliably
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Plans based on unavailable resources cannot be executed reliably

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the value of an after-action review following a major program event?
options: It captures what worked and what did not while memories are fresh
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: It captures what worked and what did not while memories are fresh

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a reasonable interpretation when outputs are on target but outcomes lag?
options: The activities may not be producing the intended change and should be examined
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The activities may not be producing the intended change and should be examined

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which is a qualitative source of evaluation evidence?
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Interviews describing participants' experiences

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which is a quantitative source of evaluation evidence?
options: Numeric counts of services delivered per month
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Numeric counts of services delivered per month

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why can combining qualitative and quantitative evidence strengthen an evaluation?
options: Numbers show the extent of change while narratives explain how and why
Only one type is ever valid
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Numbers show the extent of change while narratives explain how and why

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What should a recommendation section of an evaluation report contain?
options: Actionable suggestions that follow logically from the findings
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Actionable suggestions that follow logically from the findings

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of defining data collection responsibilities?
options: To ensure needed data are gathered consistently by accountable persons
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To ensure needed data are gathered consistently by accountable persons

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A program plan includes activities with no connection to any objective. What should a reviewer conclude?
options: Those activities may waste resources and should be justified or removed
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Those activities may waste resources and should be justified or removed

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: How do standard operating procedures support new-staff onboarding?
options: They give new staff reliable guidance on how work is performed
They are read only by managers
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They give new staff reliable guidance on how work is performed

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should evaluators guard against confirmation bias?
options: Seeking only evidence that supports expectations distorts conclusions
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Seeking only evidence that supports expectations distorts conclusions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of a communication plan for a program rollout?
options: To ensure the right information reaches the right audiences at the right time
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To ensure the right information reaches the right audiences at the right time

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a key feature of continuous improvement?
options: Regularly using performance information to make incremental refinements
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Regularly using performance information to make incremental refinements

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does effectiveness measure?
options: The degree to which intended results are achieved
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The degree to which intended results are achieved

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which planning step logically follows setting objectives?
options: Designing activities and allocating resources to achieve the objectives
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Designing activities and allocating resources to achieve the objectives

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should major program changes be documented?
options: Later analysis can account for the changes when interpreting results
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Later analysis can account for the changes when interpreting results

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a workload analysis used for in planning?
options: Estimating staffing needed to handle expected volumes of work
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Estimating staffing needed to handle expected volumes of work

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A pilot succeeded in one office but conditions differ elsewhere. What is the prudent next step?
options: Assess which conditions mattered and adapt the rollout accordingly
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Assess which conditions mattered and adapt the rollout accordingly

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why might an evaluation examine a representative sample of cases rather than every case?
options: A well-chosen sample can support sound conclusions at far lower cost and effort
Samples always give more accurate results than full counts
Samples eliminate the need for analysis
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A well-chosen sample can support sound conclusions at far lower cost and effort

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which condition most supports valid before-and-after comparisons?
options: Measuring the same indicators the same way at both points
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Measuring the same indicators the same way at both points

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is it important to distinguish symptoms from underlying causes in problem analysis?
options: Treating symptoms alone allows the underlying problem to persist
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Treating symptoms alone allows the underlying problem to persist

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the benefit of expressing targets numerically where possible?
options: Progress can be tracked precisely and objectively
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Progress can be tracked precisely and objectively

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does program integrity refer to during implementation?
options: Delivering the program as designed so results reflect the intended model
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Delivering the program as designed so results reflect the intended model

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why does poor fidelity to program design complicate evaluation?
options: Weak results may reflect poor delivery rather than a flawed model
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Weak results may reflect poor delivery rather than a flawed model

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of an issue log during implementation?
options: To record problems, assignments, and resolution status
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To record problems, assignments, and resolution status

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which approach best supports realistic budget estimates?
options: Basing estimates on actual costs of similar past activities
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Basing estimates on actual costs of similar past activities

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the primary value of program objectives to evaluators?
options: They define what success looks like and what to measure
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: They define what success looks like and what to measure

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does 'relevant' mean in the context of a SMART objective?
options: The objective contributes meaningfully to the program's goals and mission
The objective relates to any topic
The objective repeats the mission verbatim
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The objective contributes meaningfully to the program's goals and mission

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a program evaluation's audience most likely to need first?
options: A clear summary of key findings and their implications
A list of all meetings held
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A clear summary of key findings and their implications

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which practice improves the usefulness of monitoring data?
options: Reviewing it regularly and acting on what it shows
Collecting it and filing it unread
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Reviewing it regularly and acting on what it shows

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What should determine the frequency of progress reporting?
options: The pace of the work and the needs of decision-makers
The length of the report template
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The pace of the work and the needs of decision-makers

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the danger of announcing results before verification?
options: Errors may be publicized, damaging credibility and decisions
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Errors may be publicized, damaging credibility and decisions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: A unit resists providing data needed for evaluation. What is the best initial approach?
options: Explain the purpose and address the unit's concerns about the data's use
Exclude the unit from findings silently
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Explain the purpose and address the unit's concerns about the data's use

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the central idea of results-based management?
options: Decisions and resources are guided by evidence about outcomes achieved
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Decisions and resources are guided by evidence about outcomes achieved

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why must evaluators protect the confidentiality of participant information?
options: To meet ethical and legal obligations and maintain the trust needed for honest participation
To make findings impossible to verify
Confidentiality applies only to financial data
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To meet ethical and legal obligations and maintain the trust needed for honest participation

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the value of comparing planned versus actual spending during implementation?
options: Financial variances can signal problems needing management attention
It is required only at year end
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Financial variances can signal problems needing management attention

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which describes an appropriate use of client satisfaction data?
options: As one source of evidence alongside outcome and process measures
As the sole measure of program success
As data to collect but ignore
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: As one source of evidence alongside outcome and process measures

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should evaluation results be communicated differently to different audiences?
options: Decision-makers, staff, and the public need different levels of detail and emphasis to use the findings
Each audience should receive different conclusions
Only one audience may see results
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Decision-makers, staff, and the public need different levels of detail and emphasis to use the findings

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of forecasting future demand during program planning?
options: To size staffing, budgets, and capacity to the workload the program is likely to face
To guarantee demand will not change
To replace monitoring of actual demand
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To size staffing, budgets, and capacity to the workload the program is likely to face

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does the planning function of management primarily involve?
options: Setting objectives and determining how to achieve them
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Setting objectives and determining how to achieve them

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does the organizing function of management primarily involve?
options: Arranging people, tasks, and resources to carry out plans
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Arranging people, tasks, and resources to carry out plans

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why should evaluation questions be limited in number?
options: Focused questions allow deeper, more useful answers within resources
More questions always mean better evaluation
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Focused questions allow deeper, more useful answers within resources

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which describes a baseline measurement?
options: A measurement taken before the program begins or a change is made
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: A measurement taken before the program begins or a change is made

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What does trend analysis of program data reveal?
options: Direction of change over time, such as improvement or decline
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Direction of change over time, such as improvement or decline

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the appropriate response when two indicators give conflicting signals?
options: Investigate why they differ before drawing conclusions
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Investigate why they differ before drawing conclusions

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Why is it helpful to pilot data collection instruments before full use?
options: Confusing questions and errors can be fixed before affecting all data
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Confusing questions and errors can be fixed before affecting all data

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is a practical benefit of setting a review date when adopting a new procedure?
options: The procedure's performance will be formally reconsidered at a known time
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: The procedure's performance will be formally reconsidered at a known time

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the core idea of requiring programs to periodically justify their activities rather than assuming continuation?
options: Ongoing activities should demonstrate continued need and value to retain resources
All existing programs must end every year
Justification applies only to new staff
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Ongoing activities should demonstrate continued need and value to retain resources

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: Which is a warning sign that a plan's schedule is unrealistic?
options: Tasks are consistently completed late from the very start
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: Tasks are consistently completed late from the very start

program_planning_and_evaluation.question: What is the purpose of defining a minimum acceptable performance level?
options: To identify when results fall below what the agency can tolerate
program_planning_and_evaluation.answer: To identify when results fall below what the agency can tolerate
